YouTube Title: Text and Titles Appearing On Screen | ANYRVAAN Animations #1
Description: A three-minute reel of small animations, all about things arriving on screen — words, titles and shapes sliding, fading and snapping into place. The kind of motion you see in apps and videos every day, made with code. Part one of ten.

YouTube Title: Counting Numbers and Filling Bars | ANYRVAAN Animations #2
Description: Three minutes of numbers ticking up, bars filling, and meters moving in rhythm — the little animations that make data feel alive. Part two of ten.

YouTube Title: App Screens and Dashboards in Motion | ANYRVAAN Animations #3
Description: A reel of animated app screens, dashboards and buttons. The ideas here later grew into a short film called Dashboard Cinema. Part three of ten.
Inky and Printed Looks | ANYRVAAN Animations #4
Playlist: Three minutes of animation that looks drawn, inked and printed by hand rather than made on a computer. Part four of ten.

Description: Springy Motion with Weight | ANYRVAAN Animations #5
Playlist: Animations that move like they have real weight — things bouncing, springing and settling, the way real objects do. Part five of ten.

Description: Space and Orbits | ANYRVAAN Animations #6
Playlist: A reel full of starfields, orbits and the feeling of deep space and distance. Part six of ten.

YouTube Title: Glowing and Fading Light | ANYRVAAN Animations #7
Description: Three minutes of glowing, fading, ghostly light — animations about things that shine and then disappear. Part seven of ten.

YouTube Title: Patterns and Sacred Geometry | ANYRVAAN Animations #8
Description: Repeating patterns and sacred geometry, moving. This is the idea that grew into a whole calmer series of its own. Part eight of ten.

YouTube Title: Business Ideas as Little Moving Pictures | ANYRVAAN Animations #9
Description: Everyday business ideas — money, deals, growth — drawn as simple moving pictures instead of charts. Part nine of ten.

YouTube Title: The Finale: Everything Flows Together | ANYRVAAN Animations #10
Description: The last of the ten reels, where all the earlier styles come together and resolve. Part ten of ten.
Calming Pattern | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #1
Playlist: A slow, calming animation of a mandala drawing itself in light — a pattern forming around a still centre. It plays once, then again slower, so you can just watch. Part one of ten.

YouTube Title: The Yantra — Triangles and a Single Point | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #2
Description: A calm animation of interlocking triangles forming around one central point. Plays once, then slower. Part two of ten.

YouTube Title: The Ensō — One Brush Circle | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #3
Description: A single brush circle, drawn and left open. The quietest of the ten — almost nothing but the stroke and some space. Part three of ten.

YouTube Title: The Eightfold Star — An Endless Pattern | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #4
Description: Two squares turning into a star that tiles on forever. A calm, geometric animation. Part four of ten.

YouTube Title: The Rose Window — Light Through Glass | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #5
Description: A stained-glass rose window slowly coming to life in light. Part five of ten.

YouTube Title: The Tree of Life — Points and Paths | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #6
Description: Light travelling gently down a branching pattern of points and paths. Part six of ten.

YouTube Title: The Way of Water — Flowing Motion | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #7
Description: A calm animation of water splitting around a stone and joining back together. Part seven of ten.

YouTube Title: The Turning Wheel — Steady Rotation | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #8
Description: Wheels turning within wheels, calm and steady. Part eight of ten.

YouTube Title: The Endless Knot — A Line With No End | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #9
Description: One continuous line weaving a knot that never ends. Part nine of ten.

YouTube Title: The One Sound — Everything Gathers In | ANYRVAAN Calm Patterns #10
Description: The finale: the nine patterns before it gather into a single ring, then fade to silence. Part ten of ten.
The 1982 Commodore 64, recreated exactly as it looked — the startup screen, the slow tape loading, all of it. No screenshots; every pixel drawn by hand. Thirty seconds.

YouTube Title: The First Macintosh (1984) — Rebuilt From Scratch
Description: The original 1984 Macintosh screen — black and white, dragged icons, the little menus — recreated from scratch. Thirty seconds.
The old black DOS text screen, with typed commands and blue menus, recreated exactly. Thirty seconds.
Windows 95 brought back to life — the Start menu, the grey buttons, a game of Minesweeper. No screenshots, all hand-made. Thirty seconds.
The homemade web of 1997 — tiled backgrounds, a spinning UNDER CONSTRUCTION sign, and a visitor counter ticking up for you. Thirty seconds.
The Winamp music player from 1999 — green glowing clock, bouncing bars and all. Thirty seconds, drawn from scratch.
The classic Nokia 3310 — texting the slow old way, then a game of Snake. Thirty seconds.
The click-wheel iPod, scrolling through a list of songs. Thirty seconds, recreated by hand.
The BlackBerry — the trackball, the tiny keyboard, the blinking red mail light. Thirty seconds.

YouTube Title: The First iPhone (2007) — Rebuilt From Scratch
Description: The original 2007 iPhone — glossy icons and slide to unlock. The end of a 25-year journey through old screens. Thirty seconds.
Faked So Well It Looks Real
Playlist: The QuickBooks accounting app, rebuilt from scratch with a fake mouse clicking through it. It looks exactly like a screen recording — but nothing real is running. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: A spreadsheet that looks exactly like Excel — the total updating at the bottom as a fake mouse drags across the cells. All hand-made, nothing real running underneath. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: The Slack chat app, faked so well it looks like a real recording — messages arriving, a reaction ticking up as if a coworker just added it. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: Gmail, recreated from scratch — writing and sending an email, the conversation folding up afterward. Nothing real is running. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: The Stripe payments screen, faked by hand — a payment coming in, then a refund rippling through everything on the page. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: The Xero accounting app, recreated from scratch — matching up bank transactions with a click. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: Notion, faked so it looks real — ticking off a to-do list, with pages and titles and all. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: Google Calendar, recreated from scratch — creating an event and watching it drop into place on the grid. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: The VS Code editor, faked by hand — typing out code, the terminal printing results line by line. Thirty seconds.

Description: Rebuilt by Hand (Looks Real
Visibility: Trello, recreated from scratch — dragging a card from one list to another, tilt and shadow and all. Thirty seconds.

YouTube Title: A One-Minute Story Told Only by a Dashboard
Description: A short film with no words. A company's status screen quietly goes wrong, someone notices, fixes it, and it recovers — told entirely through numbers and charts on screen. Can a boring screen hold you like a movie? One minute.
